primary_reason: This review addresses diagnosis and treatment of tumefactive demyelinating lesions without an infectious-disease outcome or stated transfer route.
This seroepidemiological design estimates pertussis incidence independently of routine case reporting.
This questionnaire study descriptively assesses health-worker knowledge and reporting practices without methodological development.
This scoping review diagnoses numerator, denominator, access, and ascertainment biases in migrant infection monitoring.
This applied crowdsourcing platform provides a transferable supplementary design for foodborne-disease surveillance.
Repeated standardized point-prevalence surveys estimate healthcare-associated infections and associated patient-level factors over time.
This hospital comparison describes blood-culture capacity and utilization without a transferable estimator or evaluation method.
The study combines geonarratives, mapped environmental features, and case data to construct locally informed arbovirus risk surfaces.
This imaging study concerns complex liver cysts in polycystic kidney disease and provides no infectious-disease transfer route.
Active tracing of tuberculosis patients classified as lost to follow-up reveals outcome misclassification and underreported favorable outcomes.
This cross-sectional survey describes infection prevalence and environmental conditions without developing or evaluating a transferable epidemiological method.
The analysis validates reports, corrects underreporting through capture-recapture, and performs observed-versus-expected vaccine-safety surveillance.
This study applies catalytic force-of-infection modelling to derive age-specific incidence from seroprevalence.
This survey describes reported sexual-partnership patterns and correlates without validating or correcting the acknowledged reporting bias.
The study compares multinational hemovigilance rates across bacterial-protection systems while explicitly assessing false negatives and reporting limitations.
This infectious-process model estimates time-varying asymptomatic dengue viremia for blood-safety surveillance decisions.
This systematic review identifies recurring notification barriers and provides operational guidance for including private practitioners in disease surveillance.
This general life-expectancy analysis is dominated by noncommunicable mortality and does not specify an infectious-disease transfer route for its adjustment.
The study develops and evaluates surrogate-network reconstruction methods for correcting epidemic-risk bias caused by nonuniform contact sampling.
This diagnostic meta-analysis compares influenza-test sensitivity and specificity using hierarchical Bayesian models and evaluates bias and heterogeneity.
This study directly evaluates and revises diagnostic specimen-selection criteria that missed most giardiasis cases.
An individual-based Monte Carlo model evaluates nonpaternity and half-sibling ratios as objective estimators of underreported sexual behavior.
The study audits erroneous HIV intervention records, identifies error mechanisms, and recommends targeted reporting-quality controls and staff training.
This review summarizes healthcare-worker knowledge and risk perceptions but does not develop or substantively compare a surveillance or measurement method.
Manual linkage of two indigenous-health data sources quantifies disease-specific underreporting across gestational, congenital, and acquired syphilis.
This cluster-randomized infection-control trial preserves an informative failed primary endpoint caused by underreporting while estimating secondary intervention effects.
This cross-sectional survey describes student nurses' needlestick knowledge, exposure, and reporting without a reusable correction or validation design.
The article introduces surveillance fatigue as a recurring epidemic underreporting mechanism and supports the concept through historical examples.
Reflex molecular testing of dengue-negative outbreak samples demonstrates a transferable surveillance approach for detecting clinically similar co-circulating arboviruses.
The mixed-methods study combines case tracing, community interviews, and disability-adjusted life-year estimation while comparing passive and active surveillance yield.
The study evaluates reliability, factor structure, criterion discrimination, and proxy validity of computer-administered HIV risk-behavior instruments.
The study builds an ensemble ecological risk model from outbreak and environmental data and overlays predicted suitability with exposed populations.
This pregnancy energy-intake study addresses dietary misreporting without an infectious outcome or a concrete infectious-disease transfer route.
This study combines self-report and biomarker measurements to address exposure misclassification in HIV cohorts.
A decision-tree and Markov model projects neonatal GBS cases, deaths, disability, and costs under maternal immunization scenarios and underreporting adjustments.
The title indicates analysis of tuberculosis underreporting and HIV testing, but the absent abstract prevents judging the letter's methodological content.
This cross-sectional study estimates Leishmania prevalence and associated factors without developing or substantively evaluating a transferable epidemiological method.
Probabilistic linkage between a hepatitis cohort and national death registry directly measures cause-specific mortality underreporting while analyzing temporal trends.
This observational study reports clinical characteristics and outcomes of visceral-leishmaniasis patients with and without HIV using standard comparisons.
This serological survey documents chikungunya circulation without developing or evaluating a transferable epidemiological method.
This fetal biometric reference-chart study lacks an explicit infectious-disease epidemiological transfer route.
The cohort study validates a mobile symptom diary against conventional recording for community case detection, compliance, and questionnaire completeness.
The population-based validation study estimates sensitivity, specificity, and predictive values of self-reported treatment against a laboratory drug assay.
Individual-based epidemic simulations evaluate whether conventional observational adjustment can distinguish biological interaction from shared sexual-transmission confounding and reporting bias.
This independent-source evaluation quantifies underascertainment, underreporting, and timeliness in communicable-disease surveillance.
Two-source active surveillance with capture-recapture correction estimates vaccine-era pneumococcal incidence and distinguishes rebound from incomplete case reporting.
Community surveys and spatial mixed models estimate geographically varying rabies underreporting, correct burden projections, and inform vaccination decisions.
Hospital testing records are compared with official notification to estimate syphilis prevalence, congenital incidence, vertical transmission, and underreporting.
This national survey describes arthroplasty antibiotic and DAIR protocols but does not validate or correct registry reporting.
The study integrates human, livestock, wildlife, laboratory, and geographic records to identify surveillance gaps and cross-sector anthrax hotspots.
Population birth cohorts establish pre-Zika microcephaly baselines using standardized definitions and quantify severe underreporting relevant to outbreak surveillance.
This systematic review concerns gynecological consequences of genital cutting and supplies no explicit transferable infectious-disease methodological route.
This national surveillance summary reports healthcare-associated infection incidence and mortality without correcting the acknowledged underreporting.
A multistage probability sample of lost patients and outcome-tracing weights correct routine HIV mortality estimates and reveal site heterogeneity.
The pilot evaluates concordance between a point-of-care alcohol biomarker and self-report while identifying predictors of exposure underreporting.
This review evaluates depression treatment among people with HIV but reports no infectious or HIV-specific outcome and no methodological transfer route.
This study estimates spatiotemporal underreporting and demonstrates its consequences for reproduction-number estimation.
This study combines community and institutional active searches to quantify chikungunya underreporting.
This biomarker-validation study quantifies exposure misclassification relevant to observational HIV research.
This historical maternal-mortality study evaluates certification practices without identifying a concrete transfer of its findings to infectious-disease measurement.
This field report identifies underreporting of maternal hepatitis C and consequent infant-testing gaps.
This study begins development and evaluation of a proxy measure for underreported injection-drug exposure.
This study estimates age-specific tuberculosis incidence while correcting incomplete disaggregation and underreporting.
This advocacy article argues for congenital-toxoplasmosis screening but supplies insufficient comparative or evaluative method content.
This study uses probabilistic sample matching to address underreported sex work in HIV cascade comparisons.
This survey describes provider knowledge and reporting practices without a transferable epidemiological method.
This review estimates neuropathic pain across predominantly noninfectious neuropathies and does not provide a concrete infectious-epidemiology method.
This laboratory study develops a recombinant-protein serological assay rather than an epidemiological method.
This population survey compares biomarker-diagnosed and self-reported infections to characterize differential underreporting.
This HCV-treatment review concerns vascular adverse events rather than infection outcomes and gives no concrete infectious-method transfer route.
This study models socio-spatial determinants and underdetection of mixed-species malaria infections.
This systematic review synthesizes needlestick prevalence and reporting rates for blood-borne infection surveillance.
This study combines surveillance and registry data to assess attribution of hospital hepatitis transmission.
Linked laboratory and discharge data quantify pathogen-specific coding and diagnostic underascertainment in hospital surveillance.
This narrative review updates dengue occurrence in Nigeria and recommends better surveillance without comparing or developing surveillance methods.
This study compares parallel malaria information systems to diagnose reporting discrepancies and data-integrity failures.
This formal surveillance-system evaluation assesses usefulness, simplicity, acceptability, timeliness, stability, and representativeness.
This applied decision model evaluates the cost-effectiveness of maternal pertussis vaccination under underreporting uncertainty.
This dermatological case report concerns drug hypersensitivity without an infectious-disease epidemiological contribution.
This study describes underclaiming for infection-related patient compensation without a transferable epidemiological contribution.
This multicenter study applies standardized definitions to device-associated infection surveillance and benchmarking.
This scoping review compares transferable computational approaches for consumer-generated disease surveillance data.
This review describes Nigerian livestock arboviruses and surveillance needs without substantively comparing or prescribing a developed method.
This validation study evaluates parental recall as a transferable vaccination-coverage measurement method.
This review synthesizes validation metrics and misclassification causes in bloodstream-infection surveillance reporting.
This study compares surveillance methods and case definitions against systematic molecular respiratory testing.
This review describes Venezuelan coccidioidomycosis and proposes ecological surveillance without evaluating a transferable method.
This single feline otitis case identifies a Corynebacterium species but adds no epidemiologic method, correction, or guidance resource.
This study corrects opioid-overdose mortality coding and provides no concrete route to infectious-disease epidemiology.
This study combines spatial mapping and hierarchical regression to identify tuberculosis diagnostic-access gaps.
This broad surgical-quality review does not make infection an analytic outcome or state a concrete infectious-disease transfer route.
This study uses excess-mortality estimation and underreporting correction to assess epidemic deaths.
This systematic review compares pertussis vaccination economic models and identifies influential analytical assumptions.
This prospective cohort evaluates a transferable training intervention for pharmacovigilance reporting.
This article discusses systemic HIV data barriers without presenting a transferable analytical method or evaluation.
This study compares malaria diagnostic methods and investigates genetic causes of test failure.
This surveillance study uses paired diagnostics and capture-recapture to estimate arboviral incidence and underreporting.
This study compares mechanistic dengue model structures using statistical fit and epidemiological coherence.
This field report describes transferable outbreak surveillance, contact tracing, and data-integration practices.
This clinical review addresses multifactorial blepharitis management without an infectious-surveillance outcome or concrete epidemiologic transfer route.
This analysis validates self-reported HIV-prevention adherence against two objective drug biomarkers.
This single tinea nigra case documents an unusual diagnosis but contributes no transferable epidemiologic method or surveillance guidance.
This student survey describes knowledge and attitudes toward disease notification without evaluating reporting performance or a corrective method.
This audit measures immunization reporting accuracy and diagnoses weaknesses in data-production processes.
This laboratory biomarker study develops cellular assays without a transferable epidemiological method.
This nurse questionnaire describes occupational-exposure reporting behavior and associated factors without validating or correcting a surveillance measure.
This review describes atypical leishmaniasis distributions and parasite variants without evaluating epidemiological methods.
This systematic review diagnoses inconsistent virological-failure definitions and proposes standardized resistance-reporting criteria.
This simulation develops critical-slowing-down indicators for robust monitoring near infectious-disease elimination.
This review summarizes flaviviral ocular manifestations and laboratory pathogenesis without an epidemiologic design or transferable surveillance method.
This case review describes Philippine melioidosis burden and clinical presentations without transferable estimation methods.
This dermatology review concerns a noninfectious keratoderma and offers no concrete infectious-disease transfer route.
This outbreak study compares and mutually validates two approaches for estimating reporting completeness.
This birth-certificate validation addresses obstetric quality measurement without an explicit infectious-disease transfer route.
This analysis triangulates surveillance, serology, tick, and canine data to diagnose Lyme under-detection.
Probabilistic linkage across notification systems identifies tuberculosis underreporting and its associated factors.
This study evaluates Google Trends as an alternative real-time pertussis surveillance signal.
This evaluation quantifies hepatitis C surveillance sensitivity, false reporting, and temporal accountability.
This nursing article addresses age-related skin care rather than infectious disease and states no concrete transfer route.
This applied risk-based cost-benefit analysis compares interventions controlling zoonotic Salmonella transmission.
This four-couple HPV cancer case series raises a transmission hypothesis but adds no reusable epidemiologic design or measurement method.
This surveillance analysis compares animal-bite ascertainment and evaluates adherence to rabies prophylaxis.
This study compares surveillance notifications with hospital records and imputes unspecified bacterial etiologies.
This news report describes Europe's hepatitis challenge without sufficient methodological, diagnostic, correction, or guidance content.
This molecular survey describes febrile-illness etiologies without developing or evaluating an epidemiological method.
This cross-sectional study combines infection testing and a biomarker to assess exposure misreporting.
This outbreak analysis combines segmented regression and pair-correlation methods for cholera dynamics.
This study develops and simulates a graph-based outbreak-clustering and evidence-synthesis framework.
This neonatal bacteremia case report discusses treatment but contributes no transferable epidemiologic method or surveillance resource.
This ethical commentary concerns artificial-intelligence suicide prevention and provides no concrete infectious-disease transfer route.
This HIV-care study uses an objective biomarker to quantify alcohol-use underreporting.
This single-centre case series describes cutaneous mycobacterial presentations and outcomes without a transferable surveillance or measurement contribution.
This biobehavioral study uses hepatitis biomarkers to diagnose exposure misclassification affecting HIV transmission attribution.
This retrospective dengue study reports acute-kidney-injury prevalence and standard regression associations without a reusable measurement or correction method.
This multilevel validation identifies determinants of inaccurate parental HPV vaccination reports.
This letter supplies clinical and laboratory diagnostic characteristics intended to reduce hantavirus underdiagnosis.
This study demonstrates outcome mismeasurement from inconsistent infection definitions and reporting windows.
This analysis applies excess-mortality estimation to identify deaths missed during a chikungunya epidemic.
This sentinel-surveillance report describes French STI trends and patient characteristics without correcting the acknowledged reporting changes.
This blinded record review validates routine healthcare-associated infection surveillance and diagnoses reporting errors.
This pooled observational analysis reports HCV treatment effectiveness and safety, mentioning underreporting only as a limitation.
This study evaluates milk as an alternative sample for foot-and-mouth disease surveillance.
This review addresses outcome measurement in elderly asthma without an infectious outcome or stated infectious-disease transfer route.
This systematic review adjusts infection estimates for underreporting and quantifies previously omitted disability burden.
This dietary-questionnaire validation concerns nonalcoholic fatty liver disease and gives no concrete infectious-disease transfer route.
This record review evaluates completeness of compulsory syphilis surveillance reporting forms.
This scoping review critically appraises dengue surveillance organization, implementation, functioning, and weaknesses.
This pharmacovigilance study applies observed-to-expected analyses to evaluate a vaccine safety signal.
This single transplant-recipient HSV case illustrates an atypical presentation but adds no transferable epidemiologic method or formal guidance resource.
This healthcare-worker serosurvey describes hepatitis B prevalence, vaccination, exposures, and injury reporting using standard cross-sectional analyses.
